title: Vision Tactical
open_hours: {"Friday":["Open 24 hours"],"Monday":["Open 24 hours"],"Saturday":["Open 24 hours"],"Sunday":["Open 24 hours"],"Thursday":["Open 24 hours"],"Tuesday":["Open 24 hours"],"Wednesday":["Open 24 hours"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Vanessa Vrey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJgFlNWgeRE6ATXD0Ep8vXh0k_uEPNFrS1B-YTxL5u18vp31Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I would like to bring to your attention an incident that occurred this morning in Upper Houghton involving one of your drivers.

At the time of the incident, the traffic signal at the intersection was not functioning. As per the South African National Road Traffic Regulations, when a traffic signal is out of order it must be treated as a four-way stop, meaning that motorists must stop completely and proceed in the order in which they arrived at the intersection.

I had come to a complete stop at the intersection and began moving forward when it was my turn to proceed. At that point, one of your drivers drove through the intersection without stopping and then proceeded to behave in an aggressive and confrontational manner from within his vehicle, including shouting at me.

This conduct was both unsafe and unacceptable. In addition to failing to follow the correct procedure at a non-functional traffic light, the driver’s aggressive behaviour created an unnecessary and potentially dangerous situation.

Unfortunately, I was unable to capture the full vehicle registration number, but I recall that the number plate included the digits “22.”

I request that this matter be addressed internally and that your drivers be reminded of their responsibilities when approaching intersections where traffic signals are not operational, as well as the importance of maintaining professional and respectful conduct on the road.

. I trust that appropriate steps will be taken to ensure such behaviour does not occur again.","Images":null,"When":"3 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT25VNE9HczFUbFl3UjJKTFFuZzNTRUowU1haMmIyYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/105984195429596133388/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1773032667137769,"updated_at_unix_micros":1773032667137769,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I would like to bring to your attention an incident that occurred this morning in Upper Houghton involving one of your drivers.

Again thank you guys for the excellent service and keep going !","text_translated":"","published_at":"2026-03-11T13:06:31.03782Z"},{"Name":"Zaheerah Hoosen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXwhBp2qzXbEtuxThmNUwJWm874vrDJzU4vX-uHgKnvPeC3UBEH=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Vision Tactical oversee the security in the complex I live in. We have been having countless issues over the past months with bad management from the body corporate (which I guess is a separate issue from the security aspect) but the security guards have absolutely no concern regarding the safety of the residents in the complex. The gate and boom gate system never work. A new system was recently implemented and it’s the worst. One of the booms is broken and therefore remains permanently open. To avoid “wear and tear” on the gate, the gate stays open during the day and is only closed at night. When entering the complex, residents are expected to open their window and scan a security tag to open the boom gate and furthermore, if at night,  press the remote to open the gate. The intercom on which the scanner is on, is loose in the pavement that it is poorly bolted into and is leaning over, making it a struggle to reach. The security are constantly sitting and watching me struggle to open, having to drive as close to the pavement as I can and trying not to scrape my rims or hit my side mirror and then having to put my car in park and get half my body out the window just to scan a security tag. By the time I have done this, the gate starts closing. This evening I returned home at 11:25pm, it was raining and due to safety concerns and a 2 year old in the car, did not feel comfortable opening the window and doing all of that to try gain access to the complex (keeping in mind that the exit boom is broken and standing wide open with residents resorting to entering from that side to avoid the inconvenience of this system although I did not do so as I was just following the rules and system. The security walked out of the guard house and just stood there watching and refused to open the boom, posing a hijack risk (another resident was almost hijacked previously at this gate). Eventually after opening, he stated that it is not his job to open the boom and if we forget our tags and cannot open then only will he open. I fail to understand how this makes any logical sense? No consistency as they open for select residents but have an issue with others. Naturally, we got upset and questioned this to which the guard got annoyed and as we drove away, heard him swear at us. My husband stopped and went back to ask why he swore at us and he argued and justified his behavior stating that if we question things and get upset then he can do so. My husband said he would escalate the matter and left it at that. After getting into our apartment, my husband received a call from Vision control room and the security, Edward, who called was shouting and asking why we are harassing his guards. My husband told him that his guard swore at us and explained the situation and asked him if he is aware of the security risk and the boom which is permanently standing open. He refused to acknowledge this, continued arguing and then put the phone down. This is just one of many other security risks in the complex which are ignored by the security. From the very beginning, I noticed a significant difference in his professionalism and work ethic. He speaks fluent English, maintains eye contact, and presents himself with confidence and respect. What truly impressed me, however, was his proactive approach to security. I’ve seen him regularly patrolling the site—something I haven’t noticed with previous guards. He even checks the windows, and if he finds any open, he makes sure to close them.

When I asked him why he goes to such lengths, his answer truly stayed with me. He said: *“My job is to prevent incidents from happening. Checking windows also helps me notice if someone tries to enter the house through them.”* That level of insight and dedication is exactly what we need more of in the security industry.

Thanks to his presence, I feel safer than I ever have before. It’s clear that he takes his role seriously and that he’s been trained exceptionally well. I want to sincerely thank Vision Tactical not only for assigning such a capable guard to our site but also for the quality training you’ve provided him with.

open_hours: {"Friday":["9 am–5 pm"],"Monday":["9 am–5 pm"],"Saturday":["9 am–1 pm"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["9 am–5 pm"],"Tuesday":["9 am–5 pm"],"Wednesday":["9 am–5 pm"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Tinyiko Mathebula","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWr0BCRPVZ64C1dk-wMDRYmuOwXzf_7la7VhX0-HhaeH9UGg18nuA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I am deeply disappointed that in 2025, we still encounter individuals who cannot treat people equally and with respect.

I was at Charlotte Maxeke Hospital dropping off my mother and happened to be driving behind a white lady who was also dropping off a patient. When we were exiting, she was not asked to switch off her vehicle. However, I was instructed to do so. I refused, stating that all individuals should be treated equally. I also showed the security officer my car keys, as my vehicle is keyless.

In response, he closed the gate on me, saying I would not be allowed to leave without switching off my car, causing unnecessary traffic congestion. He then opened the oncoming gate for the cars behind me, and insisted — in isiZulu — that he would “show me who is in charge.” I felt threatened and humiliated, especially as there was no clear reason for his behavior.

I attempted to contact your office using the numbers listed on your website, but one went to voicemail and then disconnected, and the other was not helpful.

I urge you to train your personnel in proper communication, professionalism, and etiquette. This kind of unprofessional and discriminatory behavior is completely unacceptable.","Images":null,"When":"a year ago","review_id":"ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VLanZ3bzN4N2NxS0NnEAE","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/113432363814008671602/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1749455676853500,"updated_at_unix_micros":1749455676853500,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I am deeply disappointed that in 2025, we still encounter individuals who cannot treat people equally and with respect.

It was completely unprofessional and unrealistic. As a family with children aged 5, 9, and 12, we planned a special evening out before the school year started. What was meant to be a memorable night instead left my children deeply traumatized.
Just five minutes after arriving at our destination, a heavily armed security officer approached my husband, asking if the vehicle outside was his. He said there was an issue with the car. We were then informed that our vehicle had been hijacked in 2022. My husband immediately clarified that this was our vehicle, we had all the necessary documentation, and it had never been hijacked.
The situation escalated when 14 members of the JMPD, along with two security vehicles, surrounded us. They claimed our car had been used as a getaway vehicle and that my husband was being arrested. The time and date of the alleged crime were when we were on holiday in Hazyview, as confirmed by our fleet company. Despite this, the car was impounded after 4 hours.
During this ordeal, my children were left stressed, frightened, and hungry. My husband, who suffers from diabetes, high blood pressure, and high cholesterol, was unable to take his medication.
The following day, we went to the station to clear the vehicle. It was there that we learned no case existed regarding our car. The problem was due to your security company entering incorrect information into your system—wrong vehicle make, wrong number plate. Your company has had the wrong information for over three years. The fact that the police acted on this false information is unacceptable, and I am furious about the injustice you have caused to my family. Meanwhile, the real criminals are out there, unaffected.

[{"Name":"Lerato Anne","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUc9kDk1-AiHgKG3auh30g8GTQ-p48EFiST3rmtL9qFBeCil9E8GA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently had an interaction with Michael John from SCP Security, and I feel it’s important to share how outstanding his service was. Previously, I had contacted the company to enquire about their services, but unfortunately the experience was so poor that I completely lost interest.

That experience doesn’t even compare to the level of service Michael provided. He assisted both my mom and me with exceptional professionalism, kindness, and patience. Michael took the time to thoroughly explain the different packages, ensuring we fully understood our options and felt comfortable throughout the process.
His approach truly restored my confidence in the company. Michael John is a credit to SCO Security, and I highly recommend him to anyone looking for knowledgeable and genuinely caring service.
We are looking forward to a good lasting relationship.","Images":null,"When":"4 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tWT1RWTlJTVU54ZVdsSFZ6aGtTM2hWVDI5bU9GRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":5,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/114781997437090816381/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1770752162924581,"updated_at_unix_micros":1770752162924581,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I recently had an interaction with Michael John from SCP Security, and I feel it’s important to share how outstanding his service was. Previously, I had contacted the company to enquire about their services, but unfortunately the experience was so poor that I completely lost interest.

That experience doesn’t even compare to the level of service Michael provided. He assisted both my mom and me with exceptional professionalism, kindness, and patience.
